units.content: among groups formed by the convergence analyser, a subset of groups in which at least two records are produced by distinct computational measurers bearing distinct method identifiers
group contains at least one record produced by a physical measurement apparatus
convergence analyser has classified the group as converged
group is a confirmed echo chamber — convergence among distinct computational measurers is corroborated by physical measurement
convergence analyser has classified the group as tension or divergent
group is a refuted echo chamber — computational convergence is contradicted by physical measurement showing tension or divergence
group contains no record produced by a physical measurement apparatus
group is an untested echo chamber — cannot confirm or refute computational convergence without physical measurement
confirmed-, refuted-, and untested-echo-chamber signals are each significant enough to warrant routing to the actor via the feedback-signal interface
prior refuted-echo-chamber signals attributed to each measurer are accumulated over the event log
a per-measurer trust weight can be derived from the accumulation of prior refuted-echo-chamber signals attributed to that measurer
a measurer's contribution to its own measurer classification is reduced in inverse proportion to its trust weight, because repeated prior refutations lower confidence in that measurer
incorporating per-measurer trust weights derived from refutation history into the measurer-correlation analyser is a meaningful improvement to the system of claim 1
